tramplers_faq_1: What is the best Trampler for beginners?
tramplers_faq_1_answer: The Wreckage Crawler is the free starter Trampler, perfect for learning the basics. Once you are comfortable, upgrade to the Sand Stalker since its balanced stats make it forgiving for new players while still being effective in combat.
tramplers_faq_2_answer: No, you cannot switch Tramplers during a mission. You must select your Trampler before deploying. However, the Dune Fortress allows crew members to switch their individual loadouts at the mobile crafting station.
tramplers_faq_3: How do I unlock more Tramplers?
tramplers_faq_3_answer: Tramplers are unlocked using Research Materials and Building Materials earned from missions. Higher-tier Tramplers require more resources. Some special Tramplers may be available through seasonal events or promo codes.
tramplers_faq_4: What is the max crew size for a Trampler?
tramplers_faq_4_answer: Crew capacity varies by chassis: Light Tramplers hold 2 crew, Medium holds 4, and Heavy holds 6. The crew size determines how many players can operate the Trampler simultaneously.
weapons_faq_1: Should I focus on Mounted or Player weapons first?
weapons_faq_1_answer: Early game, invest in Player weapons first since they are cheaper and used on every mission. Once you have a solid Rifle and Shotgun, start upgrading your Mounted weapons for tougher encounters.
weapons_faq_2: Can I carry multiple Player weapons?
weapons_faq_2_answer: You can carry one primary weapon and one sidearm simultaneously. The sidearm slot is typically reserved for the Pistol, but other sidearms may be available through updates.
weapons_faq_3_answer: Mounted weapons have durability that degrades with use and must be repaired at the Dune Fortress crafting station. Player weapons have unlimited durability but require ammunition.
weapons_faq_4: What is the best weapon for Storm Dive?
weapons_faq_4_answer: The Railgun is the top choice for Storm Dive due to its armor penetration against elite enemies. Pair it with a Rifle as your player weapon for versatile mid-range combat.
upiors_faq_1: What is the most dangerous Upior?
upiors_faq_1_answer: The Sand Wraith is the most dangerous Upior with an Extreme threat level and S tier ranking. It should only be engaged with a full crew and proper weapon loadout. Solo encounters should be avoided entirely.
upiors_faq_2: Do Upiors respawn in the same location?
upiors_faq_2_answer: Yes, Upiors respawn at their designated spawn points after a cooldown period. High-threat Upiors like the Sand Wraith have longer respawn timers, typically 30 minutes.
upiors_faq_3: Can I avoid Upior encounters entirely?
upiors_faq_3_answer: In Voyage mode, you can often route around Upior spawn zones using the Scout Walker reconnaissance tools. In Storm Dive, Upior encounters are more frequent and harder to avoid.
upiors_faq_4: Do Upiors get stronger at night?
upiors_faq_4_answer: Yes, all Upiors gain a 20% damage and health bonus during the in-game night cycle. Plan your most dangerous encounters for daytime when possible.
tierList_faq_1: How often is the tier list updated?
tierList_faq_1_answer: We update the tier list after every major patch and balance change. Minor adjustments are made weekly based on community feedback and win-rate data from competitive play.
tierList_faq_2: Why is the Scout Walker S tier?
tierList_faq_2_answer: The Scout Walker earns S tier due to its unmatched reconnaissance value, speed, and flanking damage. In coordinated crews, information advantage is the most powerful tool, and the Scout Walker provides it in abundance.
tierList_faq_3_answer: Not at all. Lower-tier Tramplers excel in specific niches. The Ore Hauler is the best resource farmer, and the Wreckage Crawler is the only free starter option. Tier rankings reflect overall power, not absolute usefulness.
tierList_faq_4_answer: The meta shifts with each major update. Hologryph typically releases balance patches every 4-6 weeks. Sign up for our Discord to get notified about tier list changes as they happen.
gameModes_faq_1: Can I play both modes in one session?
gameModes_faq_1_answer: Yes, you can switch between Voyage and Storm Dive between missions. Your Trampler and loadout carry over, but loot and progress are tracked separately for each mode.
gameModes_faq_2_answer: Yes, Storm Dive is always PvPvE. You will encounter both hostile Upiors and competing crews. There is no PvE-only version of Storm Dive.
gameModes_faq_3: Which mode is better for beginners?
gameModes_faq_3_answer: Voyage mode is strongly recommended for beginners. It teaches core mechanics without the pressure of PvP encounters. Switch to Storm Dive once you are comfortable with extraction and combat.
gameModes_faq_4: Do rewards carry over between modes?
gameModes_faq_4_answer: Resources and building materials earned in either mode go into your shared inventory. However, some mode-specific rewards and achievements are tracked separately.
codes_faq_1: How often are new codes released?
codes_faq_1_answer: New codes are typically released every 1-2 weeks, often tied to community milestones, patch releases, or seasonal events. The official Discord is the fastest way to get notified.
codes_faq_2: Do codes work on all platforms?
codes_faq_2_answer: Currently, SAND: Raiders of Sophie is available on Steam, and all codes work on the Steam version. If the game expands to other platforms, code compatibility will be announced.
codes_faq_3_answer: No, expired codes cannot be redeemed. Once a code passes its expiration date, it is permanently disabled. Redeem codes as soon as you find them to avoid missing out.
codes_faq_4: Is there a limit to how many codes I can redeem?
codes_faq_4_answer: There is no limit to the total number of codes you can redeem, but each individual code can only be used once per account. Some codes may have global redemption caps.
